_metadata.description: Summary of compiled MKWii physics values and data availability

speed.notes: Exact km/h values for all 36 vehicles well-established from reverse engineering. Internal floating-point values need extraction from game RAM or Kinoko source.

acceleration.notes: Non-linear acceleration curve documented at sample points. Full formula requires disassembly or Kinoko source. 0-60kph times available.

mini_turbo.notes: Blue, orange, and red stages documented. Duration in 60ths of a second. Exact boost speed multiplier not precisely known. Bikes lack red stage.

handling_steering.notes: Stat point values known. Actual turning rate per frame not precisely documented. Drift types (inside/outside) well understood.

off_road.notes: Speed retention multiplier range well established.

gravity.notes: Exact value found in reverse-engineered PlayerPhysics struct: gravity = 1.3f (float32). Also a separate hopGravity field exists for hop/jump physics.

friction.notes: Per-surface type defined in KCL files, not global constants. KCL type codes documented. Exact friction coefficients per type not widely published.

item_probabilities.notes: Complete probability tables available for all player counts and positions. Multiple verified sources.

gaps_requiring_further_work: Extract exact mini-turbo speed boost multiplier per combo (PlayerBoost.multiplier field)
Document exact friction coefficients for each KCL surface type (kclSpeedFactor values)
Document the full acceleration curve formula (PlayerSub10.updateAcceleration at 0x8057b9bc)
Extract internal speed unit conversion factor (internal units to km/h)
Document trick boost speed values (PlayerZipper struct at 0x90)
Document slope physics (uphill/downhill speed effects, how floor normal affects speed)
Document collision/bump physics (mass-based knockback from PlayerSub18)
Extract hopGravity value (separate from main gravity)
Obtain Kinoko source code for cross-verification of all constants
Document KartParam.bin and BikePartsDispParam.bin stat tables for exact internal stat values

best_reference_projects.name: mkw-structures
best_reference_projects.description: Reverse-engineered C++ headers for MKWii classes (PlayerPhysics, PlayerSub10, PlayerBoost, etc.). Source of gravity=1.3 and internal state machine constants.

best_reference_projects.name: Kinoko
best_reference_projects.description: Frame-perfect MKWii physics reimplementation in C++. Primary source for exact internal constants.

best_reference_projects.name: jdaster64 FAQ
best_reference_projects.description: Comprehensive stat guide with exact top speeds and acceleration values.

best_reference_projects.name: Super Mario Files
best_reference_projects.description: Detailed stat breakdown with exact km/h speeds and mini-turbo durations.

best_reference_projects.name: Wiimms SZS Tools
best_reference_projects.description: Command-line tools for SZS/U8/BRRES/KMP files with extensive format documentation.
